steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank: a transitive verb 'used' with determiner 'the' and adjective 'chemical' requires an object noun. After the blank: an infinitive phrase of purpose ('to make a glassy solid') directly follows. The blank must contain the object noun or noun phrase.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
In Standard English, you don't place a comma between a noun or noun phrase and a following infinitive of purpose.

Option D follows this rule by joining 'compound' and 'aluminum oxide' as a single noun phrase, avoiding any comma before 'to make.' The others either place a comma before 'to' (B, C) or fail to properly close an appositive (A).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
